response:
Sec. 1  
The original task involves generating a sequence of prime numbers based on a specific rule. The sequence starts with a 5-digit prime number, and each subsequent number is the smallest prime number greater than the sum of the squares of the digits of the previous number.  

Sec. 2  
The original task requires determining the next prime number in the sequence based on the sum of the squares of the digits of the previous number. This involves calculating the sum of the squares of the digits of the initial 5-digit prime number to find the next prime.  

Sec. 3  
The original task specifies that the sequence must be generated for 7 years (ages 13 to 19). Each year, the next prime number in the sequence is calculated based on the sum of the squares of the digits of the previous prime.  

Sec. 4  
The original task requires ensuring that each prime number in the sequence is greater than the sum of the squares of the digits of the previous number. This validation step is crucial to maintain the integrity of the sequence.  

Sec. 5  
The original task emphasizes that the sequence must be generated for 7 years (ages 13 to 19), and each prime number must be the smallest prime greater than the sum of the squares of the digits of the previous number.  

Sec. 6  
The original task involves identifying the initial 5-digit prime number, which is the starting point of the sequence. This number must be a valid 5-digit prime and serve as the foundation for generating the rest of the sequence.  

Sec. 7  
The original task requires that the final response includes the prime numbers for each year in the sequence, formatted in a clear and structured manner. Each prime must be verified to ensure it adheres to the rule and is correctly calculated. player
